name: delegation
description: Time-bounded role delegation + real-time impersonation sessions. Two distinct-but-related concerns: (1) role_delegations — 'Alice is on vacation, delegate her manager role to Bob until next Friday' — a tenant User temporarily grants their role assignments to another tenant User in the same tenant, and (2) impersonation_sessions — a PlatformUser (Academorix staff) starts a short-lived act-as session for a specific tenant User, for support / debug purposes. Both concerns integrate with workflow/approvals: the approver selector engine walks role_delegations rows so a Manager's approver requirement resolves to their delegate during OOF (see access-approvals/design.md §7). Delegation is audited via HasActivityLog; impersonation is audited via a dedicated heavy trail preserved indefinitely for compliance evidence retention (SOC 2 CC6.1 + CC6.7, PCI-DSS 8.5). Impersonation session records survive the impersonator's PlatformUser offboarding — the row keeps an anonymised impersonator_display_name + impersonator_department snapshot after the platform_users row hard-deletes at the end of its 90-day retention hold (see platform-user/module.json). The impersonation Sanctum PAT is issued by identity/auth's ImpersonateStartAction (start-point per identity/design.md §12) and is a NEW PAT scoped to the impersonation — never the impersonator's own PlatformUser PAT — so on session end the token dies without invalidating the operator's regular platform-admin session.
consumed_by_note: workflow/approvals reads role_delegations rows via DelegationResolver during approver-selector evaluation (see access-approvals/design.md §7). Domain modules never call the resolver directly — they call Gate::allows() and the RBAC PermissionResolver traverses delegation transparently.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing role-delegation CRUD. A delegator invites another User in the same tenant to hold their role for a bounded window. Delegate + delegator can both view; only delegator (or admin) can revoke early. Cross-tenant delegation is refused at the observer (see rules.json + observers.json).

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin impersonation surface. `POST /impersonation/start` composes identity/auth's ImpersonateStartAction (which mints the impersonation-scoped Sanctum PAT) and writes the impersonation_sessions row atomically. Every read + terminate action is heavily audited. `force-terminate` is reserved for security-lockdown scenarios — always a super_admin or security role.

compliance_note: SOC 2 CC6.1 + CC6.7: every impersonation session logged with impersonator + impersonated + reason + duration; audit trail preserved indefinitely (7 years hot per retention.json) even when the impersonator's PlatformUser row is hard-deleted post 90-day retention hold. PCI-DSS 8.5: MFA required for impersonation start (inherited from platform_admin guard's MFA-mandatory policy per platform-user/middleware.json). GDPR Art. 17: impersonation records cascade on target User Identity erasure via CASCADE on impersonated_user_id, but impersonator's actions retained as compliance evidence (justified under Art. 17(3)(e) — legal claims / defence).
cross_plane_note: role_delegations rows are TENANT-plane — both delegator + delegate are tenant Users, both same tenant_id, both same application_id. impersonation_sessions rows are CROSS-PLANE — impersonator is a PlatformUser (central-plane, no tenant_id) and impersonated is a tenant User (with tenant_id + application_id). This module is the ONE place in the tenant plane where a central-plane FK (impersonator_platform_user_id → platform_users.id) is permitted; the platform_users row belongs to the impersonation as the actor of record. See tenancy-columns.md §5 exception for platform-plane FKs on tenant-plane rows tracking cross-plane operations.
